sanskrit:
अध्येष्यते च य इमं धर्म्यं संवादमावयोः।
ज्ञानयज्ञेन तेनाहमिष्टः स्यामिति मे मतिः॥
transliteration: adhyeṣyate ca ya imaṃ dharmyaṃ saṃvādam āvayoḥ | jñāna-yajñena tenāham iṣṭaḥ syām iti me matiḥ ||
literal_meaning: Whoever will study this sacred dialogue of ours, by the sacrifice of knowledge, that person has worshipped Me—this is My conviction.
contextual_meaning: Krishna closes the Gītā by reframing attentive study itself as an inner ritual: when the mind becomes the altar and understanding becomes the offering, the student is no longer separate from the Divine.
psychological_state: Post-insight consolidation—mind has moved from doubt to embodied comprehension and now seeks to anchor the insight through repetition and reflection.
trigger_scenario: Re-reading a pivotal self-help or therapy note months later and realizing the words have become part of your automatic self-talk.
anchor_text: Study with full attention; your understanding itself is the worship.
